new_classes.definition_en: Other grape must (heading 2204 30) that has been concentrated, as defined by Additional Note 7 to Chapter 22 of the CN. This subheading covers grape must other than that in fermentation or with fermentation arrested otherwise than by addition of alcohol (subheading 2204 30 10), which has been concentrated.

new_classes.label_en: Concentrated quality (grape must reduced by evaporation or other means)
new_classes.label_de: Konzentrierungsqualität (durch Verdampfung oder andere Verfahren konzentrierter Traubenmost)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by grape must that has undergone a concentration process (e.g. vacuum evaporation) reducing its water content, thereby increasing its sugar and total dry matter content, as per Additional Note 7 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criterion — that this is *concentrated* grape must falling under CN 2204 30 (other grape must, i.e. not in fermentation/arrested without alcohol) — is captured by the new class with its ConcentratedQuality restriction and the complement restriction against the fermentation-arrested sibling (2204 30 10). However, the legal note explicitly refers to Additional Note 7 to Chapter 22, which defines precisely what "concentrated" means (conditions on Brix/sugar content or total dry matter). Since Additional Note 7 is not reproduced in the provided legal text and no corresponding TBox individuals or classes encode its specific thresholds, the detailed numeric criteria cannot be fully axiomatised. Coverage is therefore set to 0.75: the categorical distinction (concentrated vs. not concentrated, concentrated grape must vs. in-fermentation must) is captured, but the numeric concentration thresholds from Additional Note 7 are not.
